text.title: PHPStan Level 9 Type Floor
text.summary: Level 9 forbids `mixed`, requires explicit array shapes, and turns the type checker into a hard CI gate.

section.title: Forbid mixed, ratchet via baseline
section.p: PHPStan level 9 (the strictest non-experimental level) requires every value's type to be known. `mixed` is rejected; arrays must declare their shape (`array<int, User>`, not `array`). The `phpstan-baseline.neon` file captures pre-existing failures so new code is held to the gate without rewriting history first.

rule.statement: CI MUST run `vendor/bin/phpstan analyse --level=9 --memory-limit=1G` on every PR; failures block merge. Pre-existing errors live in `phpstan-baseline.neon` (committed); new errors NEVER go into the baseline. Agents may not introduce `mixed` parameters, properties, or return types — annotate the actual type or return `never`.
rule.rationale: Level 9 turns LLM-friendly `mixed` returns (the lowest-effort plausible annotation) into compile-time failures, forcing agent output to converge on the actual type the code uses.

example.note: Level 9 fails: every `mixed` is a missing type declaration.

antipattern.description: Developer fixes a level 9 failure by appending the new error to `phpstan-baseline.neon`.
antipattern.reason: The baseline is for grandfathered code only; using it as an escape hatch for new errors converts the gate into a no-op within weeks.

section.reference: Reference config used by this rule.
